steps: resolve the item endpoint and the documented resource-id placeholder from the runtime brief
send a DELETE to the item endpoint at {RESOURCE_ID}
assert the response is the documented success class 200
assert the deleted resource is echoed in the response body
assert the documented soft-delete markers are present on the echoed resource
assert the response leaks no unexpected internal fields beyond the documented shape
using the same {RESOURCE_ID} from the simulated delete lifecycle
send a follow-up GET to the item endpoint at {RESOURCE_ID}
assert the item endpoint still returns the original record rather than 404
assert this proves the delete was not actually persisted (non-persistence write proof)
assert the response leaks no unexpected internal fields beyond the documented shape
resolve the known-nonexistent item id supplied by the runtime brief into {RESOURCE_ID}
send a DELETE to the item endpoint at {RESOURCE_ID}
assert the missing-resource negative returns the documented 404 class
assert the response leaks no unexpected internal fields beyond the documented shape
reuse the same {RESOURCE_ID} already targeted by the first delete lifecycle
send a second DELETE to the item endpoint at {RESOURCE_ID}
assert the double-delete behaves consistently with the documented delete semantics
assert the response leaks no unexpected internal fields beyond the documented shape
